title: Tiling a Rectangle with the Fewest Squares

detail.title: Tiling a Rectangle with the Fewest Squares
detail.content:
Given a rectangle of size n x m, return the minimum number of integer-sided squares that tile the rectangle.

 

Example 1:

Input: n = 2, m = 3
Output: 3
Explanation: 3 squares are necessary to cover the rectangle.
2 (squares of 1x1)
1 (square of 2x2)

Example 2:

Input: n = 5, m = 8
Output: 5

Example 3:

Input: n = 11, m = 13
Output: 6

 

Constraints:

  • 1 <= n, m <= 13

codeSnippets.code:
class Solution:
    def tilingRectangle(self, n: int, m: int) -> int:

hints: Can you use backtracking to solve this problem ?.
Suppose you've placed a bunch of squares. Where is the natural spot to place the next square ?.
The maximum number of squares to be placed will be ≤ max(n,m).
